regime_justification: USDJPY at 158.52, back within 18 pips of May 1 intervention trigger (160), yen surrendering half its intervention gains; JGB at 27-year high; US rates sticky after inflation surge; BOJ split on timing of next hike

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY retracing to 158.5 (18 pips from MOF watch-line at 160) after April 30 intervention spent ~$35bn; MOF official cited IMF rule noting only 2 more 3-day intervention windows remain by Nov; vol bid; JGB yields at 27-year highs

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: USDJPY retest of 160 watch-line probable; MOF intervention bullets limited (2 left); JGB at 2.63%, oil at $102

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: BOJ June 15-16 meeting risk; spread at 187 bps still wide; oil-driven ¥/bbl at 16.2k yen pressuring import costs

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: If USDJPY retests 160, MOF may exhaust intervention capacity by mid-June; BOJ meeting decision critical

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Scenario-weighted: if BOJ signals hike or Hormuz resolves, pressure eases; unwind risk diminishes unless policy stalemate persists

bottom_line: Yen carry unwind pressure has receded from April 30 spike but not extinguished. USDJPY at 158.5 — just 18 pips from the MOF's 160 trigger — after surrendering half of intervention-driven gains. Japan spent ~$35bn propping up yen but MOF official noted only 2 intervention windows remain by Nov under IMF rules. JGB 10Y hit 2.63% (highest since 1997), US 10Y at 4.49%, spread 187 bps. Crude $102, ¥/bbl at 16,200 stoking import inflation. BOJ April minutes showed hawkish split (3 dissents for 1.0% hike), next meeting June 15-16. MOVE at 70, VIX at 17.9 — both subdued. BTC steady at $80.5k. Grade 1-2w C (watchful): intervention ammunition running low, USDJPY pace reaccelerating, BOJ decision risk looms. Grade 3w C on meeting risk. Grade 4w D if BOJ signals credible tightening path or geopolitical backdrop improves. Setup remains fragile; complacency in vol despite thin MOF firepower.

Next meeting Jun 15-16; April meeting saw 3 dissents for 1.0%; minutes showed hawkish tilt but majority cautious on Iran risk

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: JGB 10Y at 2.63%, highest since 1997; already above 2020s cycle high; market pricing BOJ hike trajectory
MOVE at 70; calm despite JGB breakout and US inflation surge; complacency or confidence in policy response
WTI $102, Hormuz closed since Mar 1; ¥/bbl at 16,245 yen stoking Japan import inflation; Trump-Xi talks stalled

narrative.p1: Overnight: USDJPY climbed to 158.52 (+0.10%), retracing within 18 pips of the 160 level that triggered MOF intervention on April 30. The yen has now surrendered roughly half of the ~3% surge it printed after Japan reportedly spent $35 billion propping up the currency two weeks ago. A MOF official on Monday cited IMF rules noting that three consecutive days of intervention count as one episode, and that Japan has only 2 such windows remaining by November if it wants to retain its free-float status. JGB 10Y held at 2.63%, a 27-year high, as markets price in BOJ hiking risk following April minutes that showed three board members dissented for a 1.0% rate. US 10Y at 4.49% after hot CPI/PPI prints linked to Iran war oil shock (WTI $102, up 65% YoY). MOVE at 70 and VIX at 17.9 — both subdued. BTC steady at $80.5k. No BOJ/MOF headlines in the last 24 hours; market focus on Trump-Xi meeting and Hormuz status. Stage unchanged at 2 (Pre-Intervention): USDJPY pace reaccelerating toward MOF watch-line, vol bid, intervention firepower dwindling.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread at 187 bps (US 4.49%, JGB 2.63%), up 6 bps today as US yields sticky post-inflation surge. Carry-to-vol proxy ~23 (187 bps ÷ 8% implied vol) — still attractive for carry but vol too low given proximity to intervention zone. USDJPY 5-day move +1.7% (reacceleration after intervention); MOF historically reacts to pace, not just level. ¥/bbl crude at 16,245 yen (WTI $102 × 158.5), up 65% YoY, stoking Japan import inflation and complicating BOJ policy. USDJPY 18 pips from 160, well within intraday noise — retests probable. Distance from 60d mean: compressed (USDJPY drifting back toward pre-intervention levels). Grade 1-2w C: Applied unwind prior as tiebreaker — data is mixed (vol low, spread wide, but intervention capacity limited and JGBs breaking out). Without the prior, would grade D (minor pressure, contained). With the prior, C (watchful) given MOF bullet scarcity, BOJ meeting June 15-16, and USDJPY retracement setup. Grade 3w C on BOJ decision risk. Grade 4w D: scenario-weighted toward policy resolution (BOJ hike signal or Hormuz ceasefire) easing pressure by mid-June.
narrative.p3: Crypto liquidity: BTC at $80,481, up 1.2% on the day, consolidating the $79-82k range after ETF inflows of $131m on May 14 (BlackRock IBIT led with $144m). Open interest stable; no capitulation. BTC holding up despite rates backup and oil shock signals risk-on appetite persists — equities grinding higher (S&P +0.8% today), credit spreads tight. XRP and alts tracking BTC. For positioning: BTC's resilience in a rising-rate, rising-oil environment suggests either (a) crypto has decoupled from macro stress or (b) complacency is mispriced. If yen carry unwinds hard (USDJPY sub-150, vol spike), expect crypto liquidity to drain as levered positions unwind. Current setup: BTC range-bound, waiting for macro catalyst. No immediate crypto unwind signal, but watch BTC if USDJPY retests 160 and MOF exhausts intervention capacity — forced deleveraging would hit crypto alongside equities.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1-2w both graded C. USDJPY at 158.5, just 18 pips from 160, with 5-day pace reaccelerating (+1.7%). MOF has 2 intervention bullets left; if USDJPY retests 160 in next week, MOF may fire again, but effectiveness questionable given wide rate differential and oil pressure. BOJ meeting June 15-16 is 4 weeks out, so not immediate catalyst, but April minutes showed hawkish split (3 dissents). JGB at 27-year high pricing hike risk. MOVE and VIX subdued, but that's complacency, not all-clear. Unwind prior applied as tiebreaker: would otherwise grade D (pressure contained), but thin MOF firepower and JGB breakout tilt to C.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2w to 3w: sustained C. If USDJPY retests 160 by late May, MOF may exhaust second intervention window, leaving 1 bullet by early June. BOJ meeting June 15-16 becomes focal point. If BOJ holds at 0.75% and signals caution, yen weakens further and carry persists (grade stays C-D). If BOJ surprises with hike or accelerated normalization signal, yen rallies and unwind risk spikes (grade moves to B). Scenario probability: 60% no hike, 30% 25bp hike, 10% larger move. Weighted outcome keeps grade at C.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3w to 4w: C → D. By week 4 (mid-June), BOJ decision will be known. Base case: BOJ hikes 25bp or signals credible tightening path, which would ease intervention pressure and stabilize USDJPY in 150s, reducing unwind risk (grade D). Alternative: BOJ holds, USDJPY breaks 160 again, MOF exhausts final bullet, yen capitulates to 165+ and unwind begins in earnest (grade B-A). Also watching Hormuz: if Trump-Xi talks produce ceasefire framework, oil collapses, ¥/bbl pressure eases, BOJ buys time. Scenario-weighted toward partial resolution by late June, hence grade D. Non-monotonic path (C/C/C/D) reflects catalysts clustering in weeks 2-3 with resolution bias by week 4.

watch_list: USDJPY retest of 160 level (18 pips away as of May 15 close)
BOJ June 15-16 meeting decision and forward guidance on policy rate trajectory
MOF second intervention: if triggered, leaves only 1 IMF-compliant window by Nov
WTI crude and Hormuz status: Trump-Xi talks outcome, ceasefire framework progress
JGB 10Y above 2.65%: would reinforce market pricing of imminent BOJ hike, tighten financial conditions
